``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined Wisconsin has created prison-release avenues that place trial judges in the position of back-end decision-makers. Sentencing courts have unusual powers to vary the degrees of indeterminacy in their sentences. They also have sentence “modification” and “adjustment” powers that can be exercised long into the life of a prison sentence. Prison sentences are bifurcated into an initial “term of confinement” and a separate term of post-release supervision called the “extended supervision term.” In most cases, the timespan of the confinement term may never exceed 75 percent of the judicial maximum term (“total bifurcated sentence”) and the extended supervision term may not be less than 25 percent of the total.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were people in prison past their parole eligibility year. This group made up of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

## Missing Data This section will contain information related to missingness in the NCRP data. Keep?


# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 11%, changing from 22,724 in 2010 to 20,202 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series

# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases decreased 3%, dropping from 9,444 in 2010 to 9,155 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison reveal notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and black, non-hispanic people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age-wise, the majority of people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates In Wisconsin, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 14 times higher and Hispanic, any race people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 2.7 times higher and Other race(s), non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 2.7 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served ::: {.cell} ::: Hispanic, any race individuals faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 3.5 years. Other race(s), non-Hispanic individuals experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 1.8 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For Murder and Non-negligent Manslaughter offenses, Hispanic, any race individuals had 11.4 more years on average compared to Other race(s), non-Hispanic individuals, who had the shortest time served for these offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
